Vidyadharas (the learned ?) in battle.
[Bhattotpala interprets the word Vidyndharanam,a,s DevayoninZm
meaning demigods. However, since the author is explaining
effects of the transit on the denizens of the earth, and not ofheaven,
that word must stand for some class of human beings of learned
men.]
When Venus stays in the constellation Aslesa, he will cause
acute suffering to the people through snakes. But when he pierces

Course of Venus IX 107
the star Magha, he will harm the commanders of elephant-divisions
or chief ministers, and ensure copious rains.
is another reading for The piercing or occul-
tation of the junction star of Magha by a planet is defined by
astronomers as follows : "A planet occults the junction star of
Magha (Regulus) when its own rectified latitude, being in the
same direction (north), is more than half the latitude, and less than
one and a half the latitude of the star." Dr. Kern continues to
